items.evidence: Transcript context: 'so I go to California thinking that I'm going to with this other woman Becky Mills that we're going to start an STS [SDS] chapter. And we just somehow never get it off the ground...' This is the speaker's would-be SDS-Berkeley co-organizer at UC Berkeley in fall 1961 / 1962. Whisper consistently mishears 'SDS' as 'STS' in this transcript, but the name 'Becky Mills' is rendered consistently in the single occurrence in-transcript. The Pass 2 hypothesis 'Becky Adams (likely Adams-Mills)' has no external corroboration in SNCC Digital Gateway, CRMVet, Library of Congress finding aids, the UBalt Betty Garman Robinson Papers, or web search for SDS Berkeley 1961-62 organizers. Ground-truth corpus (civil_rights_facts.json) contains no Mills/Adams/Adams-Mills entry. The 'Adams-Mills' hyphenation appears to be Pass 2 speculation, not derived from a citable source. Retain the literal Whisper rendering 'Becky Mills' as the safest candidate; flag the surname as unverified pending archival lookup (e.g., UBalt SDS chapter records, Skidmore alumni records, or Garman Robinson's personal papers).
